data.title: merge-to-main.sh fails when main has uncommitted tracked-file changes from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var
data.description: When CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT points to the main repo (not the worktree), hooks that load library files from the main repo can leave uncommitted tracked-file changes on the main branch. merge-to-main.sh then fails with 'Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by merge' during the merge phase. Root cause: dso-setup.sh, hook-error-handler.sh, or other scripts edited directly in the main repo (via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT resolution) while the worktree holds the same file — creating a dirty main branch that blocks merge. Also, local main can diverge from origin/main when a previous merge-to-main run created a version bump commit that was never pushed. Mitigation: reset local main to origin/main before retry. Fix: merge-to-main.sh should stash or detect dirty state on main before merging, and/or the workflow should prevent edits to plugin files via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT in the main repo during a worktree session.
